400.full_name: al-Bara' bin Azib bin al-Harith b. 'Ady b. Jushum b. Majda'a b. Haritha b. al-Harith b. al-Khazraj
400.mother_name: Umm Habiba bint Abi Habiba/Umm Khalid bint Thabit

401.full_name: 'Ubaid bin Azib bin al-Harith b. 'Ady b. Jushum b. Majda'a b. Haritha b. al-Harith b. al-Khazraj
401.mother_name: Umm Habiba bint Abi Habiba/Umm Khalid bint Thabit

402.full_name: 'Uqba bin 'Amr bin Tha'laba b. Usayra b. 'Usayra (b. 'Atiyya) b. Jadara b. 'Auf b. al-Harith
402.arabic: أبو مسعود الأنصاري البدري ,عقبة بن عمر
402.bio: He was the youngest person at the second Aqaba. He was may be too young to participate at Badr.

403.full_name: Sahl bin Hunayf  b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash b. 'Auf
403.bio:
Full Lineage: Sahl bin Hunayf b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Malik b. al-Aus
Other Kunya : Abu Thabit, Abu Sa'id, Abu al-Walid

404.full_name: 'Uthman bin Hunayf  b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash
404.bio: Full Lineage: 'Uthman bin Hunayf b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Malik b. al-Aus

405.full_name: As'ad bin Sahl bin Hunayf  b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr
405.arabic: أسعد أبو أمامة بن سهل بن حنيف
405.bio:
Full Lineage: As'ad bin Sahl bin Hunayf b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr (Bahzaj) b. Hanash b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Malik b. al-Aus
His name was chosen by Prophet(ﷺ) based on his grandfather (mother side), As'ad bin Zurara (Abu Umama)

406.full_name: 'Abbad  bin Hunayf  b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash
406.bio: Full Lineage: 'Abbad bin Hunayf b. Wahib b. al-'Ukaym b. Tha'laba b. al-Harith b. Majda'a b. 'Amr b. Hanash b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Malik b. al-Aus

407.full_name: 'Amir bin Qays bin Saleem bin Hadar bin Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udhr
407.bio:
Full Lineage: Abu Burda ibn Qays bin Saleem bin Hadar bin Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udar b. Wa'il b. Najia b. al-Jumahar b. Asha'r b. Udd
He may be one of the emigrants to Abyssinia who later moved to Medina in 2 boats in 8 AH.

408.full_name: 'Ubaid bin Saleem bin Hadar bin Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udhr
408.bio: Full Lineage: 'Ubaid bin Saleem bin Hadar bin Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udar b. Wa'il b. Najia b. al-Jumahar b. Asha'r b. Udd

409.full_name: Ibrahim bin Abi Musa bin Qays bin Saleem b. Hadar b. Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udhr
409.bio: Full Lineage: Ibrahim bin Abi Musa ibn Qays bin Saleem bin Hadar bin Harb b. 'Amir b. 'Unz b. Bakr b. 'Amir b. 'Udar b. Wa'il b. Najia b. al-Jumahar b. Asha'r b. Udd

410.full_name: Khuzayma  bin Thabit bin al-Fakih b. Tha'laba b. Sa'ada b. 'Amir b. Ghiyan b. 'Amir b. Khutama
410.bio:
Full Lineage: Khuzayma bin Thabit bin al-Fakih b. Tha'laba b. Sa'ada b. 'Amir b. Ghiyan b. 'Amir b. Khutama ('Abdullah bin Jushum b. Malik b. al-Aus)
His Testimony was made equivalent to two men by prophet(ﷺ).

411.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Thabit bin al-Fakih b. Tha'laba b. Sa'ada b. 'Amir b. Ghiyan b. 'Amir b. Khutama
411.arabic: عبد الله بن ثابت بن الفاكه الأنصاري
411.bio: Full Lineage: 'Abdullah bin Thabit bin al-Fakih b. Tha'laba b. Sa'ada b. 'Amir b. Ghiyan b. 'Amir b. Khutama ('Abdullah bin Jushum b. Malik b. al-Aus)

412.full_name: 'Amara bin Aws bin Khalid b. 'Ubiad b. Umayya b. 'Amir b. Khutama
412.mother_name: Safiyya bint Ka'b bin Malik of Ghatafan
412.bio:
Full Lineage: 'Amara bin Aws bin Khalid b. 'Ubiad b. Umayya b. 'Amir b. Khutama ('Abdullah bin Jushum b. Malik b. al-Aus)
عمارة بن أوس بن خالد بن عبيد بن أمية بن عامر بن خطمة

413.full_name: Muhammad bin 'Abdullah bin Jahsh bin Ri'ab b. Ya'mar b. sabra b. Murra b. Kabir b. Ghanm b. Dudan
413.arabic: محمد بن عبد الله بن جحش

414.full_name: Fatima bint Abi Hubaysh(Qays) bin al-Muttalib b. Asad b. 'Abdul-'Uzza b. Qua'syy b. Kilab b. Murra
414.father_name: Abu Hubaysh(Qays) bin al-Muttalib b. Asad

415.full_name: Amina bint Ruqaysh b. Ri'ab b. Ya'mar b. Sabra b. Murra b. Kabir b. Ghanm b. Dudan b. Asad

416.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Ruqaysh b. Ri'ab b. Ya'mar b. Sabra b. Murra b. Kabir b. Ghanm b. Dudan b. Asad
416.bio: Ally of B. 'Abdu Shams from B. Asad b. Khuzayma.

417.full_name: Rayta bint Munabbih bin al-Hajjaj b. 'Amir b. Hudhayfa b. Sa'd b. Sahm b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b

418.full_name: Umm 'Abdulla bint Milhan b. Khalid b. Zayd b. Haram b. Jundub b. 'Amir b. Ghanm b. 'Ady b. al-Najjar
418.father_name: Malik b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haram
418.mother_name: Muliakah bint Malik bin 'Adi b. Zaid Manat

419.full_name: al-'Aas bin  Suhayl bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir
419.arabic: أبو جندل بن سهيل بن عمرو
419.mother_name: Fakhitah bint 'Amir b. Nawfal b. 'Abd Manaf
419.bio: He joined Muslim at Hudaybia against his father's will. His father chained him in the house.

420.full_name: Khalid ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah
420.mother_name: Fakhtah bint Zuhayr b. al-Harith b. Asad
420.bio: He was Killed on his way to Abyssinia.

421.full_name: Juwayriyya bint Abi Jahl b. Hisham b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum
421.father_name: Abu Jahl ('Amr) b. Hisham b. al-Mughirah Makhzumi
421.bio: 'Ali (RA) wants to marry her during the life of Prophet(ﷺ) but he disapproves it.

422.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Zayd bin Sahl b. al-Aswad b. Haram b. 'Amr b. Zayd Manat b. 'Adi b. 'Amr b. Malik

423.full_name: Hakim ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b
423.mother_name: Fakhtah bint Zuhayr b. al-Harith b. Asad
423.bio: He lived for 120 years. He became Muslim on day of Conquest of Makkah.

424.full_name: Al-Hunfa'  bint Abi Jahl b. Hisham b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah
424.father_name: Abu Jahl ('Amr) b. Hisham b. al-Mughirah Makhzumi

425.full_name: Hatib bin Abi Balta'ah bin 'Amr b. 'Umayr b. Salma b. Sa'b al-Lakhmi
425.father_name: Rashida bin Azabb b. Jazila b. lakhum
425.bio:
Ally of B. Asad.
He was sent by Muhammad with a letter to Muqawqis, an Egyptian Coptic Christian official. He returned with gifts, including two slaves, Maria al-Qibtiyya and her sister Sirin. Muhammad took Maria as his wife.
A veteran of the Battle of Badr, it was discovered that he had sent a secret letter to the Quraish detailing Muhammad's movements. When confronted, he begged mercy explaining that he had only hoped the powerful tribe would help protect his family in return. While Umar ibn Khattab sought the Prophet's permission to kill Balta'ah, Muhammad said that it was unnecessary, as God has forgiven all veterans of Badr, and knew the sincerity of his heart. The following was revealed after the incident:
O you who believe! Take not My enemies and your enemies as protecting friends showing affection towards them, while they have disbelieved in what has come to you of the truth....". [60:1]"

426.full_name: Arwa bint Kuraiz bin Rabi'a b. Habib b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Manaf b. Quassy b. Kilab b. Murrah
426.father_name: Kuraiz bin Rabi'ah b. Habib b. 'Abd Shams
426.spouses: Affan ibn Abi al-'As,'Utba bin Abi Mu'iat

427.full_name: 'Amir bin Kuraiz bin Rabi'a b. Habib b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Manaf b. Quassy b. Kilab b. Murrah
427.father_name: Kuraiz bin Rabi'ah b. Habib b. 'Abd Shams
427.bio: His son was governor of Basrah for 'Uthman bin 'Affan

428.full_name: 'Utba bin Abi Lahab ibn 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah

429.full_name: Dihya bin Khalifa b. Farwah b. Fadalah b. Zayd b. Imri'ul-Qays b. al-Khazraj al-kalbi al-Qudha'i
429.bio: The Jabreel sometimes come in the shape of him. He was sent to Caesar, the emperor of the Rome.

430.full_name: Mu'attib bin Abi Lahab ibn 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
430.arabic: معتب بن أبي لهب بن عبد المطلب بن هاشم
430.mother_name: Umm Jamil(Hind) bint Harb bin Umayya
430.bio: Lost his Eye in the battle of Hunain.

431.full_name: Zaynab bint al-'Awwam bin Khuwaylid bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah
431.arabic: زينب بنت العوام بن خويلد بن أسد القرشية
431.bio: Her husband and 4 sons became Muslim on the day of Conquest of Makkah.

432.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Hakim ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b
432.arabic: عبد الله بن حكيم بن حزام القرشي الأسدي
432.bio: He became Muslim on day of Conquest of Makkah.

433.full_name: Khalid bin Hakim ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b
433.arabic: خالد بن حكيم بن حزام القرشي الأسدي
433.bio: He became Muslim on day of Conquest of Makkah.

434.full_name: Hisham bin Hakim ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b
434.arabic: هشام بن حكيم بن حزام القرشي الأسدي
434.bio: He became Muslim on day of Conquest of Makkah. He died before his Father.

435.full_name: Yahya bin Hakim ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b
435.arabic: يحيى بن حكيم بن حزام القرشي الأسدي
435.bio: He became Muslim on day of Conquest of Makkah.

436.short_name: Umm Habib bint al-'Awwam bin Khuwaylid
436.full_name: Umm Habib bint al-'Awwam bin Khuwaylid bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah
436.arabic: أم حبيب بنت العوام بن خويلد القرشية
436.bio: Her husband died on his way to Abyssinia during first migration.

437.short_name: Umm al-Hasan bint Khalid ibn Hizam
437.full_name: Umm al-Hasan bint Khalid ibn Hizam bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah
437.arabic: أم الحسن بنت خالد بن حرام بن خويلد
437.bio: His father was Killed on his way to Abyssinia.

438.full_name: Safinah (Mahran bin Faroukh or Ruman), Client of Umm Salama, servant of Prophet (saw)
438.arabic: سفينة مولى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم
438.bio: He was freed by Umm Salama to serve Prophet(ﷺ).

439.full_name: al-Saib bin al-'Awwam bin Khuwaylid bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah

441.full_name: Rayta/Rabta/Fatima bint Sufyan bin al-Harith b. Umayya b. al-Fadhal b. Munqaf b. 'Afif of Khuz'a
441.arabic: رابطة بنت سفيان بن الحارث الخزاعية

442.full_name: 'Ai'sha bint Qudamah bin Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
442.arabic: عائشة بنت قدامة بن مظعون الجمحي

443.full_name: Zaynab bint Uthman bin Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
443.arabic: زينب بنت عثمان بن مظعون الجمحية

444.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman ibn Uthman bin Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
444.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن عثمان بن مظعون الجمحي

445.full_name: Safiyahbint al-Khattab b. Nufayl b. 'Abd al-Uzza b. Riya'h b. 'Abdullah b. Qurt b. Razah b. 'Adi
445.arabic: صفية بنت الخطاب بن نفيل القرشية العدوية
445.mother_name: Hantamah bint Hisham b. Mughira Makhzumi

446.full_name: Khawla (b. Malik) bint Tha'laba b. Asram b. Fihr b. Qays b. Tha'laba b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf
446.bio:
The beginning of Surah Al-Mujadilah (The Woman who disputes) was revealed about her and her husband.
1. Indeed Allah has heard the statement of her (Khaulah bint Tha'labah) that disputes with you (O Muhammad ) concerning her husband (Aus bin As-Samit), and complains to Allah. And Allah hears the argument between you both. Verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Seer. 2. Those among you who make their wives unlawful (Az-Zihar) to them by saying to them You are like my mother's back." They cannot be their mothers. None can be their mothers except those who gave them birth. And verily, they utter an ill word and a lie. And verily, Allah is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving. 3. And those who make unlawful to them (their wives) (by Az-Zihar) and wish to free themselves from what they uttered, (the penalty) in that case (is) the freeing of a slave before they touch each other. That is an admonition to you (so that you may not return to such an ill thing). And Allah is All-Aware of what you do. 4. And he who finds not (the money for freeing a slave) must fast two successive months before they both touch each other. And for him who is unable to do so, he should feed sixty of Miskin (poor). That is in order that you may have perfect Faith in Allah and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allah. And for disbelievers, there is a painful torment. 5. Verily, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad ) will be disgraced, as those before them (among the past nation), were disgraced. And We have sent down clear Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). And for the disbelievers is a disgracing torment."

448.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum
448.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن الحارث بن هشام

449.full_name: Safwan bin Umayyah b. Khalaf b. Wahb b. Hudhafah b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays
449.bio: Safwan's wife, Najiyah, converted to Islam before her husband, after the conquest of Makka. Safwan ibn Umayya fled, but Muhammed sent Wahb ibn Umayr after him, with Muhammed's cloak as token of safe-conduct. Safwan returned and became friends with Muhammed, although he did not convert to Islam until after the battles of Hunayn and at-Ta'if. (Al Muwatta Hadith 28.44)

450.full_name: al-Sakran bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
450.arabic: السكران بن عمرو بن عبد شمس

451.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Hassan ibn Thabit b. al-Mandhur b. Haram b. 'Amr b. Zayd Manat b. 'Adi b. 'Amr
451.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن حسان بن ثابت

452.full_name: Hassan bin Thabit b. al-Mandhur b. Haram b. 'Amr b. Zayd Manat b. 'Adi b. 'Amr b. Malik b. al-Najjar
452.bio: He lived for about 120 years. He was one of the Prophet(ﷺ) poet.

453.full_name: Durrah/Fakhita bint Abi Lahab ibn 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab
453.arabic: درة بنت أبي لهب بن عبد المطلب الهاشمية
453.mother_name: Umm Jamil(Hind) bint Harb bin Umayya
453.bio: Her husband al-Harith bin 'Amir bin Nawfal was killed at Badr.

454.full_name: Fatima bint al-Walid b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum
454.bio: She became Muslim on the day of opening of Makkah.

455.full_name: Salma bin Nuaym ibn Mas'ud b. 'Amir b. Unayf b. Tha'laba b. Qunfadh b. Khalawa b. Sabi' b. Bakr
455.arabic: سلمة بن نعيم بن مسعود الأشجعي
455.teachers: من لقي الله لا يشرك به شيئا دخل الجنة,أبيه نعيم

456.short_name: Umm Sabir bint Nua'ym ibn Mas'ud
456.full_name: Umm Sabir bint Nuaym ibn Mas'ud b. 'Amir b. Unayf b. Tha'laba b. Qunfadh b. Khalawa b. Sabi' b. Bakr
456.arabic: أم صابر بنت نعيم بن مسعود الأشجعي

457.full_name: Zayd bin Sohan b. Hajar b. al-Harith b. al-Hajrus b. Sabra b. Hadrjan b. 'Asas b. Laith al-'Abdi

458.full_name: Sihan bin Sohan b. Hajar b. al-Harith b. al-Hajrus b. Sabra b. Hadrjan b. 'Asas b. Laith al-'Abdi

459.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Zayd b. al-Khattab b. Nufayl b. 'Abd al-Uzza b. Riyah b. Abdullah b. Qurt b. Razah
459.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن زيد بن الخطاب العدوي
459.bio: His original name was Muhammad which was changed by 'Umar bin al-Khattab. He was governor of Makkah for Yazid but later removed by Yazid.
459.spouses: Umm 'Amara bint Sufyan,10578,Maymuna bint Basr,Sawda bint 'Abdullah

460.full_name: Sahel bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
460.arabic: سهل بن عمرو بن عبد شمس العامري

461.full_name: Hatib/Abu Hatib bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
461.arabic: حاطب بن عمرو بن عبد شمس
461.spouses: Rayta bint 'alqamah b. 'Abdullah b. Abi Qays

462.full_name: Saleet bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
462.arabic: سليط بن عمرو بن عبد شمس العامري
462.mother_name: Khuli bint 'Amr b. al-Harith b. 'Amr from Yemen
462.bio: He was Prophet(ﷺ) messenger to Hawdhah bin 'Ali Al-Hanafi at al-Yamamah

463.full_name: Nuaym ibn Mas'ud b. 'Amir b. Unayf b. Tha'laba b. Qunfadh b. Khalawa b. Sabi' b. Bakr b. Ashja'
463.bio:
Nuaym ibn Masud was from Najd in the northern highlands of Arabia. He belonged to the powerful Ghatafan tribe. As a young man, he was clever and alert. He was full of enterprise and travelled widely. He was resourceful, every ready to take up a challenge and not prepared to allow any problem to get the better of him.
While in Yathrib, Nuaym was known to spend generously and he in turn would be lavishly entertained. In this way Nuaym came to develop strong links among the Jews of the city and in particular with the Banu Qurayzah.
He played an important part in the battle of Khandaq.

464.full_name: Rabi'ah ibn Ka'b bin Malik bin Ya'mar al-Aslami Hijazi
464.arabic: ربيعة بن كعب بن مالك الأسلمي
464.father_name: Ka'b bin Malik bin Ya'mar al-Aslami
464.bio: He used to sleep at the door of Prophet(ﷺ) house. He also provide the water for Wudu. He was from the companions of Suffa.

465.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman b. Sakran b. 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Luayy

466.full_name: Sa'sa'a bin Sohan b. Hajar b. al-Harith b. al-Hajrus b. Sabra b. Hadrjan b. 'Asas b. Laith al-'Abdi

467.full_name: Wahb bin 'Umayr bin Wahb b. Khalaf b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
467.arabic: وهب بن عمير بن وهب بن خلف
467.bio: He participated at Badr from Quraish Side and was captured. His father came to kill Prophet(ﷺ) in Medina but he became Muslim.

468.full_name: Qays bin 'Amr bin Qays bin Zaid bin Sawad b. Malik b. Ghanm b. Malik b. al-Najjar
468.bio: His participation at Badr is not agreed upon.

469.full_name: 'Amr bin Qays bin Zayd bin Sawad b. Malik b. Ghanm b. Malik b. al-Najjar
469.bio: His participation at Badr is not agreed upon.

470.full_name: Qudamah bin Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
470.father_name: Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa
470.mother_name: Gahazia bint Hawrith b. 'Unais b. Wahaban b. Wahb
470.bio: Participated in all major battles. He was Governor of Bahrain during 'Umar's Khilafat.

471.full_name: Suraqa bin Malik bin Ja'sham b. Malik b. 'Amr b. Taym b. Mudlaj b. Murra b. 'Abd Manat b. Kinana
471.arabic: سراقة بن مالك بن جعشم الكناني

472.full_name: Shuja bin Wahb  b. Rabi'a b. Asad b. Suhayb b. Malik b. kabir b. Ghanm b. Dudan b. Asad b. Khuzima
472.bio: Ally of B. 'Abdu Shams from B. Asad b. Khuzayma. He was sent to the Ghassani king in Balqa', Syria.

473.full_name: Asma' bint Zayd b. al-Khattab b. Nufayl b. 'Abd al-Uzza b. Riyah b. Abdullah b. Qurt b. Razah
473.arabic: أسماء بنت زيد بن الخطاب العدوي

474.full_name: al-Sa'ib ibn Uthman bin Maz'un b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
474.arabic: السائب بن عثمان بن مظعون الجمحي
474.bio: Participated in Battles with Prophet(ﷺ). Brotherhood was made between him and Harithah bin Saraqah Ansari.

475.arabic: رافع مولى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم

476.arabic: شقران مولى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم

478.arabic: أنسة مولى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم

479.full_name: Aslam or Ibrahim Abu Rafi', Client of Prophet (saw)
479.arabic: أسلم أبو رافع مولى رسول الله
479.bio: He was slave of al-'Abbas and was given to Prophet (ﷺ) as gift. He was set free when al-'Abbas converted to Islam.

480.short_name: Dhakwan or Kisan or Tahman or Mihran
480.full_name: Dhakwan or Kisan or Tahman or Mihran
480.arabic: ذكوان مولى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم

481.full_name: al-Aswad bin Sufyan bin 'Abd al-Asad bin Hilal b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah b. Murrah
481.bio: According to some sources his father was killed in the battle of Badr as a disbeliever and he himself was taken as POW.

482.full_name: Ahmed/'Abdul Hamid bin Hafs b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum
482.arabic: أبو عمرو بن حفص بن المغيرة
482.bio: His name is also mentioned as Abu Hafs bin 'Amr bin  al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum

483.full_name: 'Ady ibn al-Rabi' bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin 'Abd Shams bin 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
483.arabic: عدي بن الربيع بن عبد العزي بن عبد شمس

484.full_name: Zurara ibn 'Umair b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. 'Abd al-Dar b. Qusayy b. Kilab
484.arabic: أبو عزيز بن عمير بن هاشم
484.bio: According to many scholars, He died as a pagan. His son became Muslim.

485.full_name: Ma'di- Karib bin Qays b. Ma'di-Karib b. Mau'wiya b. Jabla b. 'Aidy b. Rabi'a al-Kundi
485.arabic: الأشعث بن قيس بن معدي كرب الكندي
485.bio: He was governor for 'Uthman bin Affan (RA) and general for Ali (RA). According to some sources, he apostate d after Prophet(ﷺ) death but later revert back to Islam

486.full_name: Ibrahim ibn Qays b. Ma'di-Karib b. Mau'wiya b. Jabla b. 'Aidy b. Rabi'a al-Kundi

487.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Safwan bin Umayyah b. Khalaf b. Wahb b. Hudhafah b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays
487.arabic: عبد الله بن صفوان بن أمية
487.bio: He was killed with Ibn Zubair in Makkah.

488.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Safwan bin Umayyah b. Khalaf b. Wahb b. Hudhafah b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays
488.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن صفوان بن أمية الجمحي
488.bio: He was killed with Ibn Zubair in Makkah
488.teachers: أنه استعار من أبيه صفوان درعا

489.full_name: Barza bint Mad'ud ibn 'Amr bin 'Umair al-Thaqafiyya
489.arabic: برزة بنت مسعود بن عمرو الثقفي

490.full_name: Halah bint Khuwaylid bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab (Tahira)

491.full_name: Hizam bin Khuwaylid bin Asad bin 'Abdul-'Uzza bin Quasyy bin Kilab b. Murrah
491.spouses: Umm Hakim (Fakhtah bint Zuhayr b. al-Harith b. Asad)

492.full_name: Qays ibn Hudhaifa b. Qays b. 'Adi b. Sa'd b. Sahm b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b b. Lu'ayy

493.full_name: Haram bin Milhan b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haraam b. Jundub b. 'Amir b. Ghanm b. 'Adi b. al-Najjar
493.father_name: Malik b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haraam
493.mother_name: Muliakah bint Malik bin 'Adi b. Zaid Manat

494.full_name: al-Baghum bint al-Mu'adhil (Khalid) bin 'Amr b. Sufyan b. al-Harith b. Zaban of Kinana

495.full_name: Sulaym bin Milhan b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haraam b. Jundub b. 'Amir b. Ghanm b. 'Adi b. al-Najjar
495.father_name: Malik b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haraam
495.mother_name: Muliakah bint Malik bin 'Adi b. Zaid Manat

496.full_name: Zayd al-Khayl bin Muhalhil b. Zayd b. Munhab b. 'Abd Rada b. Afsi b. al-Mukhtalas b. Thobe al-Tayyi'
496.bio:
When Zayd al-Khayl heard the news of the Prophet, peace be upon him, he made some of his own
enquiries and then decided to go to Madinah to meet the Prophet. With him was a big delegation of his
people among whom were Zurr ibn Sudoos, Malik ibn Jubayr, Aamir ibn Duwayn and others.
When they reached Madinah, they went straight to the Prophet's Mosque and tethered their
mounts at its door. It happened that as they entered, the Prophet was on the mimbar addressing the
Muslims. His speech aroused Zayd and his delegation and they were also astonished by the rapt
attention of the Muslims and the effect of the Prophet's words on them. The Prophet was saying:
I am better for you than al-Uzza (one of the main idols of the Arabs in Jahiliyyah) and everything
else that you worship. I am better for you than the black camel which you worship besides God."
The Prophet's words had two different effects on Zayd al-Khayl and those with him. Some of
them responded positively to the Truth and accepted it. Some turned away and rejected it. One of the
latter was Zurr ibn Sudoos. When he saw the devotion of the believers to Muhammad, both envy and
fear filled his heart and he said to those with him:
"I see a man who shall certainly captivate all Arabs and bring them under his sway. I shall not let
him control me ever." He then headed towards Syria where it is said he shaved his head (as was the
practice of some monks) and became a Christian.
The reaction of Zayd and others was different. When the Prophet had finished speaking, Zayd
stood up, tall and impressive-looking in the midst of the Muslims and said in a loud and clear voice:
"O Muhammad, I testify that there is no god but Allah and that you are the messenger of Allah."
The Prophet came up to him and asked, "Who are you?"
"I am Zayd al-Khayl the son of Muhalhil."
"From now on you are Zayd al-Khayr instead, not Zayd al-Khayl," said the Prophet. "Praise be to
God Who has brought you from the hills and dales of your native land and softened your heart towards
Islam." Thereafter he was known as Zayd al-Khayr (Zayd the Good).
The Prophet then took him to his house. With them were Umar ibn al-Khattab and some other
Companions. The Prophet gave him a cushion to sit on but he felt very uncomfortable to recline thus in
the presence of the Prophet and he returned the cushion. The Prophet handed it back to him and he
returned it to him. This happened three times. Eventually, when they were all seated, the Prophet said to
Zayd al-Khayr:
"O Zayd, no man has ever been described to me and when I see him he does not fit the description
at all except you. You have two characteristics which are pleasing to God and His Prophet."
"What are they?" asked Zayd.
"Perseverance and sagacity," replied the Prophet.
"Praise be to God," said Zayd, "Who has given me what He and His Prophet like." He then turned
directly to the Prophet and said:
"Give me, O rnessenger of God, three hundred horsemen and I promise you that I will secure
Byzantine territory with them."
The Prophet praised his fervour and said, "What manner of man are you!"
During this visit, all those who stayed with Zayd became Muslims. They then desired to return to
their homes in Najd and the Prophet bade them farewell. The great desire of Zayd al-Khayr to work and
fight for the cause of Islam, however, was not to be realised.
In Madinah al-Munawwarah at that time there was an epidemic of fever and Zayd al-Khayr
succumbed to it and said to those with him: "Take me away from the land of Qays. I have the fever of
small pox. By God, I shall not fight as a Muslim before I meet Allah, the Mighty the Great."
Zayd took the road to his people in Najd in spite of the fact that the fever became more and more
intense and slowed him down. He hoped at least to get back to his people and that they would become
Muslims, through God's grace, at his hands. He struggled to overcome the fever but it got the better of
him and he breathed his last on the way before reaching Najd. Between his acceptance of Islam and his
death, however, there was no time for him to have fallen into sin."

497.arabic: ريطة بنت عبد الله بن معاوية الثقفية
497.bio: The mother of Ibn Mas'ud's child. She was a craftswoman.

498.full_name: Zaynab bint. Abi Mu'awiya (or Mu'awiya) al-Thaqafiyyah

499.full_name: Umm 'Abd bint 'Abd Wadd b. Sawa b. Quraym b. Sahilah b. Kahil b. al-Harith
499.mother_name: Hind  bint 'Abd b. al-Harith b. Zuhrah b. Kilab
